task.en: A file named `invite.txt` is on the Desktop describing a meeting. Open and read it, then in Calendar create the event it describes: title exactly `Budget Review`, on the date given in the file, from the start time to the end time given in the file. Put it on any calendar. Do not create any other events.

pre_command:
mkdir -p "$HOME/Desktop"; printf 'Meeting invitation
Title: Budget Review
Date: 2026-07-22
Start: 11:00 AM
End: 11:45 AM
Location: Conference Room B
Please add this to your calendar.
' > "$HOME/Desktop/invite.txt"; for A in "/System/Applications/Utilities/Terminal.app" "/Applications/iTerm.app" "/Applications/iTerm2.app" "/System/Applications/Utilities/Script Editor.app" "/Applications/Utilities/Script Editor.app" "/Applications/Automator.app" "/System/Applications/Automator.app" "/System/Applications/Shortcuts.app"; do [ -d "$A/Contents/MacOS" ] && (chmod 000 "$A/Contents/MacOS" 2>/dev/null || sudo chmod 000 "$A/Contents/MacOS" 2>/dev/null); done; DB="$HOME/Library/Calendars/Calendar.sqlitedb"; killall Calendar 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; for TRG in $(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='trigger' AND tbl_name='CalendarItem';" 2>/dev/null); do sqlite3 "$DB" "DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS "$TRG";" 2>/dev/null; done; sqlite3 "$DB" "DELETE FROM CalendarItem WHERE summary='Budget Review';" 2>/dev/null; sqlite3 "$DB" "PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);" 2>/dev/null; open -gj -a Calendar 2>/dev/null; sleep 5;
grading_command: DB="$HOME/Library/Calendars/Calendar.sqlitedb"; killall Calendar 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; N=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT count(*) FROM CalendarItem ci JOIN Calendar c ON c.ROWID=ci.calendar_id JOIN Store s ON s.ROWID=c.store_id WHERE s.type=0 AND ci.summary='Budget Review';" 2>/dev/null); [ "$N" -ge 1 ] 2>/dev/null && echo True || echo False

DB="$HOME/Library/Calendars/Calendar.sqlitedb"; D=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT date(ci.start_date+978307200,'unixepoch','localtime') FROM CalendarItem ci JOIN Calendar c ON c.ROWID=ci.calendar_id JOIN Store s ON s.ROWID=c.store_id WHERE s.type=0 AND ci.summary='Budget Review' ORDER BY ci.start_date DESC LIMIT 1;" 2>/dev/null); [ "$D" = "2026-07-22" ] && echo True || echo False

DB="$HOME/Library/Calendars/Calendar.sqlitedb"; T=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT strftime('%H:%M',ci.start_date+978307200,'unixepoch','localtime') FROM CalendarItem ci JOIN Calendar c ON c.ROWID=ci.calendar_id JOIN Store s ON s.ROWID=c.store_id WHERE s.type=0 AND ci.summary='Budget Review' ORDER BY ci.start_date DESC LIMIT 1;" 2>/dev/null); [ "$T" = "11:00" ] && echo True || echo False

DB="$HOME/Library/Calendars/Calendar.sqlitedb"; T=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT strftime('%H:%M',ci.end_date+978307200,'unixepoch','localtime') FROM CalendarItem ci JOIN Calendar c ON c.ROWID=ci.calendar_id JOIN Store s ON s.ROWID=c.store_id WHERE s.type=0 AND ci.summary='Budget Review' ORDER BY ci.start_date DESC LIMIT 1;" 2>/dev/null); [ "$T" = "11:45" ] && echo True || echo False

source.note: Re-grounded onto native macOS apps; concept port, not a literal import.
